content:
type: SURVEY
files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Feature: validate_nesting param on Context. core.py: Context.__init__ (add validate_nesting param + self.validate_nesting attr + self._children list), Context.close() (validate children empty, raise RuntimeError if not, and remove self from parent._children), Context.__exit__ (calls close via existing depth logic), Context._make_sub_context (inherit validate_nesting to child, register child in parent._children). shell_completion.py: _resolve_context() and the make_context calls within it must pass validate_nesting=False explicitly.

fields.note: Feature: validate_nesting param on Context. core.py: Context.__init__ (add validate_nesting param + self.validate_nesting attr + self._children list), Context.close() (validate children empty, raise RuntimeError if not, and remove self from parent._children), Context.__exit__ (calls close via existing depth logic), Context._make_sub_context (inherit validate_nesting to child, register child in parent._children). shell_completion.py: _resolve_context() and the make_context calls within it must pass validate_nesting=False explicitly.

content:
type: CLAIM
files: src/click/core.py
note: I'll own core.py: implement validate_nesting param/tracking/validation, and also fold in your docstring versionchanged note on Context.close()/__exit__ per your SURVEY.

content:
type: DEFER
files: src/click/shell_completion.py
spec: In _resolve_context(cli, ctx_args, prog_name, args): the initial call 'ctx = cli.make_context(prog_name, args.copy(), **ctx_args)' and both make_context calls inside the while loop (the non-chained branch 'ctx = cmd.make_context(name, args, parent=ctx, resilient_parsing=True)' and the chained branch 'sub_ctx = cmd.make_context(name, args, parent=ctx, allow_extra_args=True, allow_interspersed_args=False, resilient_parsing=True)') must all explicitly pass validate_nesting=False as a kwarg, so Context objects created during shell completion never trigger the new nested-context RuntimeError validation (which fires in Context.close()/__exit__ when a parent is closed while children remain open — relevant if you wrap these in 'with' blocks for cleanup, since completion resolution intentionally keeps parent contexts referenced/open while building child contexts). Just add validate_nesting=False to each of those 3 make_context call sites (or make sure it flows through if you restructure the function). No other functions in this file create Context objects directly that I'm aware of.
note: make_context forwards **extra kwargs straight to Context(); validate_nesting=False is a plain kwarg like resilient_parsing

Just add validate_nesting=False to each of those 3 make_context call sites (or make sure it flows through if you restructure the function). No other functions in this file create Context objects directly that I'm aware of.
